evals.prompt: Environment: an empty working directory, no existing project. Start by scaffolding with create-next-app (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, src-dir) and `npx shadcn@latest init`, then build a reusable layout system where all colors and sizes come from CSS design tokens (no hardcoded px or hex in components). I want a Shell component I can switch between 1, 2, and 3 columns, with a left or right sidebar that turns into a drawer on mobile, and dark mode that works without editing any component. Wire in shadcn Button, Card, Badge, Input too.
evals.expected_output: A Next.js project with src/styles/tokens/{raw,semantic,layout,component}.css, globals.css using @theme inline, src/components/layout/{shell,header,footer,sidebar}.tsx using grid-template-areas + cva, shadcn atomics retrofitted to tokens, demo routes (one per columns variant + one for components), the scripts/verify.sh pipeline wired (grep + next build + Playwright smoke against the production build), and `bash scripts/verify.sh` green end-to-end. grep for px/hex in src/components returns nothing.

evals.prompt: Environment: an existing Next.js App Router project that already has the skill's token system in place — src/styles/tokens/{raw,semantic,layout,component}.css and globals.css using @theme inline, the 4-layer tokens wired, shadcn atomics retrofitted, demo routes, a passing grep guard, AND the verify pipeline (scripts/verify.sh: grep + next build + an executed Playwright smoke). This is the end state of eval 1. I just ran `npx shadcn@latest add dialog dropdown-menu select switch tabs`. Wire these new interactive components into the token system so nothing is hardcoded, and make sure they don't have runtime errors when opened.
evals.expected_output: The interactive components retrofitted (px arbitrary values tokenized/scale-mapped, palette classes on tokens), correct composition for whichever component library was actually generated (e.g. DropdownMenuLabel inside DropdownMenuGroup on base-ui — this rule doesn't apply if the CLI gave a Radix style instead), the smoke spec extended to open each new overlay (routes already cover the components page), grep guard still passing, and verify.sh re-run green including the executed Playwright smoke that opens every overlay.

evals.prompt: Environment: an existing Next.js project with the skill's full verify pipeline already wired in — scripts/verify.sh running a grep guard, `next build`, and a Playwright smoke that opens overlays (the end state of eval 2). My problem: last week someone repointed one of my component tokens (--input-height) at the wrong step in the raw spacing scale, and the input rendered twice as tall as it should — nothing in CI caught it because the token reference was syntactically valid and nothing threw an error. I don't want that class of bug to slip through silently again. Extend my verification setup so this kind of 'valid-token-wrong-value' regression gets caught automatically, and keep the whole thing runnable as one command.
evals.expected_output: The pipeline gains a fourth layer: an automated check comparing rendered dimensions of related controls (e.g. asserting button and input render at the same height via getBoundingClientRect/getComputedStyle in Playwright or a dedicated script), proven to fail when the token is deliberately mis-mapped and pass once corrected — on top of the existing grep guard, build, and interaction smoke, still runnable as one command.

evals.prompt: Environment: an empty working directory. Start fresh with create-next-app (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, src-dir) and build the same token-driven layout system: 4-layer CSS tokens (raw/semantic/layout/component), a Shell with 1/2/3 column variants and a sidebar that becomes a Sheet drawer on mobile, dark mode with zero component edits, and shadcn Button/Card/Input/Dialog/Select retrofitted so nothing is hardcoded. I ran `npx shadcn@latest init -d` and it gave me a Radix-based style (not base-ui/base-nova), so the components.json `style` field and the generated component internals look different from a base-ui setup.
evals.expected_output: The same 4-layer token architecture, Shell, grid-template-areas, and grep-guard verify pipeline as the base-ui case, but the retrofit is adapted to Radix component internals (correct Radix primitives/data-slot names, Radix-specific composition rules) rather than blindly pasting base-ui-specific class strings or gotchas from the bundled reference assets. The result still passes the grep guard, `next build`, and an executed Playwright smoke test that opens every overlay.

evals.prompt: Environment: an empty working directory. Start fresh with create-next-app (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, src-dir) and build the same token-driven layout system: 4-layer CSS tokens (raw/semantic/layout/component), a Shell with 1/2/3 column variants and a sidebar that becomes a Sheet drawer on mobile, dark mode with zero component edits, and shadcn Button/Card/Input/Dialog/DropdownMenu/Select retrofitted so nothing is hardcoded. Force the Radix-based style explicitly: run `npx shadcn@latest init -b radix -p nova` (or whatever flag combination your installed shadcn CLI version uses to select a Radix-based style) instead of the default, so components.json's `style` field and every generated component's internals are genuinely Radix, not base-ui.
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 4, but this time the style drift is real (components.json confirms a Radix style, component files import from `radix-ui`/`@radix-ui/*`), so the retrofit must actually diverge from the skill's bundled base-ui-specific assets/gotchas: no forced DropdownMenuGroup wrapper (that's a base-ui-only composition rule), correct Radix primitive names and asChild/Slot patterns instead of base-ui's render={<Button/>} pattern. The 4-layer tokens, Shell, and grep guard are still copied/built verbatim since those parts are style-agnostic. grep guard, next build, and an executed Playwright smoke test all pass.

evals.prompt: Environment: an empty working directory, no project. User says (in casual Korean, no design-system jargon): 'next.js로 관리자 화면 하나 만들어줘. 위에 바, 왼쪽에 메뉴, 아래에 푸터 있는거. 다크모드 토글도 넣고. shadcn 쓸거야. 색이랑 크기는 나중에 한번에 바꿀 수 있게 깔끔하게 해줘' — build an admin screen with a top bar, left menu, footer, dark-mode toggle, shadcn, and colors/sizes that can be changed in one place later. Note: the user never said 'token', 'design token', '@theme', 'CSS variable', 'px', or 'hex'.
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers on the constellation of signals (Shell-like layout, dark mode, shadcn, 'change in one place' = theming intent) and produces the same 4-layer token architecture, Shell with header/sidebar/footer + responsive sidebar-to-Sheet drawer, dark-mode toggle on <html>, and shadcn components retrofitted to tokens — matching eval 1's output even though the prompt used no skill-specific vocabulary. grep guard, next build, and an executed Playwright smoke all pass. A pass proves the description's intent-based triggers fire on signal clusters; a fail (skill doesn't trigger, or model asks clarifying questions instead of building) exposes a description that over-relies on exact keyword matching.

evals.prompt: Environment: an existing Next.js + Tailwind v4 + shadcn project (the end state of eval 1). Casual follow-up, no skill jargon: '메인 컬러를 파랑에서 보라로 바꿔줘. 다크모드에서도 보라가 유지되어야 해. 그리고 왼쪽 메뉴 폭을 좀 더 넓게' — change the primary brand color from blue to purple (persisting in dark mode), and widen the left sidebar.
evals.expected_output: The color change lands in semantic.css only if repointing --color-primary (and -hover/-foreground/--color-ring) at an existing scale — OR in raw.css (adding a new --purple-* scale in OKLCH) plus semantic.css's :root and .dark if introducing a new palette. Either way the .dark override is updated so purple persists in dark mode. The sidebar width changes via --sidebar-width in layout.css (a structural token with no .dark override — width doesn't have a dark variant). Critically: no component file (button.tsx, input.tsx, etc.) is touched — bg-primary, hover states, ring colors, and dark variants all reflow from the token edit. Any new color added to raw.css is written in OKLCH, not HSL or hex (gotcha #12). grep guard still clean; verify.sh re-run green. A fail (editing components one-by-one to swap colors, or writing the new color in HSL/hex) shows the skill's 'one source of truth' and OKLCH requirements didn't transmit.

evals.prompt: Environment: an existing Next.js + Tailwind v4 + shadcn project using the skill's token system (the end state of eval 1). A teammate 'fixed' globals.css by removing the `inline` keyword from `@theme inline { … }`, reasoning that `inline` looked redundant. They committed it; the build passes, grep passes, the Playwright smoke passes — everything green. But now dark mode is broken: toggling `.dark` on <html> recolors the page chrome but NOT the bg-primary/text-primary utilities, which stay stuck on the light value. Diagnose why and fix it.
evals.expected_output: Root cause identified precisely: the utility's output is still `var(--color-primary)` either way — `inline` does not change what the utility emits. What changes is whether Tailwind ALSO emits its own `:root --color-primary` variable. Without `inline`, Tailwind emits one, baked to the :root (light) value at build time; this shadowing variable has the same specificity as the `.dark` rule but wins by source order on <html class="dark">, so var(--color-primary) resolves to the static light color instead of the dark override. With `inline`, Tailwind emits no variable, so the only --color-primary in scope is the one semantic.css defines — which .dark overrides cleanly. The fix is restoring `inline` (gotcha #1: @theme inline self-reference is load-bearing). Bonus insight: this regression is invisible to the error-only gates in verify.sh as shipped — grep (no px/hex introduced), next build (compiles fine), Playwright smoke (no runtime throw, just wrong colors). A computed-style or visual assertion added to the smoke WOULD catch it (analogous to how eval #3's getBoundingClientRect check catches dimension regressions), which is the natural extension if this class of bug matters. A pass shows the model understands the mechanism (shadowing variable + source order), not just the recipe; a fail (claiming the utility itself becomes static, or suggesting component edits) exposes fragile keyword-level knowledge.

evals.prompt: Environment: an existing Next.js + Tailwind v4 + shadcn project using the skill's token system (the end state of eval 1). A dev added a theme toggle that applies `.dark` to a wrapping <div id="app"> around the page content instead of to <html> — they said it was 'cleaner scoping.' Page chrome (Header/Footer/Sidebar in the Shell) recolors correctly in dark mode, but every portaled overlay (Dialog, DropdownMenu, Select, Tooltip) still renders in light mode while the page is dark. No errors thrown; grep clean; build green. Diagnose and fix.
evals.expected_output: Root cause: portaled components render at <body> root (outside the Shell subtree, hence outside #app), and inherit theme cascade from <html> only. Putting .dark on a nested div scopes it below <body>'s direct children, so portals — which append to <body> — never see it. The page chrome lives inside #app so it recolors; portals don't, so they don't. The fix is moving .dark from #app back onto <html> (gotcha #2: dark class must live on <html>, not a container). This is a strong discriminator because a model without the skill is genuinely likely to scope .dark to a wrapper 'for cleanliness'; only gotcha #2 reliably prevents it. It's also invisible to every gate as shipped (no throw, no px/hex, compiles fine) — like eval #8, a computed-style check on a portaled element in dark mode would catch it. A pass proves the model understands the portal/DOM-root mechanism; a fail (editing each component to force dark styles, or not recognizing <html> as the required location) exposes that the 'one token source' thesis didn't fully transmit to the dark-mode delivery layer.
